rulings.Case Title: DEBBIE RAMIREZ et al VS. CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, SEPTEMBER 4, 2007, LINE 4. DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL, SCOTT MCFARLANE AND MATHEW WEBER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: Hearing required. Does it make a difference in the duty analysis whether the C.H.P. moved the vehicle or directed Ramirez to move the vehicle or acquiesced in the vehicle being moved? If the Court concludes the evidence supports the proposition that the young men were intoxicated when left in the parking lot, does that preclude the granting of summary judgment? Does plaintiff contend that the police were obligated to take the passengers into custody? If so, what is the authority for that proposition? If not, were the police obligated to wait for the brother to arrive and pick them up? What is the authority for that proposition? What evidence is there that the parking lot was more dangerous than any other location in the Ingleside neighborhood but for the police station? Is there any evidence apart from the surrounding trees and the fact that the level of light is uncertain? What legal or factual support is there for the proposition that being shot in the middle of the night is reasonably foreseeable? =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: JEROME GROESHL et al VS. AC AND S, INC., et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, SEPTEMBER 4, 2007, LINE 5. DEFENDANT GRAYBAR ELECTRIC COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF. ALTHOUGH DECEDENT IDENTIFIED DEFENDANT'S PRODUCTS, DECEDENT'S TESTIMONY REFLECTS THAT HE HAD NO SPECIFIC INFORMATION ABOUT DEFENDANT'S PRODUCTS BEING PRESENT ON THE NAVY VESSELS AND AT THE SHIPYARD WHERE PLAINTIFF WORKED. (SEE DEPOSITION AT 132 THROUGH 133, 266 THROUGH 267, 331 THROUGH 332 AND 485 THROUGH 486.) PLAINTIFF'S EXPERT DECLARATIONS FAIL TO PRESENT A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT BECAUSE THEY RELY ON PLAINTIFF'S INADEQUATE TESTIMONY. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO MEET THE DUE DELIGENCE STANDARD OF 437(C). =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: FRANCIS COLLINS VS. BENJAMIN KAPLAN
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, SEPTEMBER 4, 2007, LINE 8. DEFENDANT BENJAMIN KAPLAN'S (D.B.A. KAPLAN & SAM) DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. DEMURRER IS BASED ON C.C.P. SECTION 430.10(D) FOR MISJOINDER. DEFENDANT CAN BE INDEMNIFIED IF NECESSARY BY FILING A CROSS-COMPLAINT. OVERRULE DEMURRER BASED ON C.C.P. SECTION 430.10 (E) WHICH WAS BASED ON 430.10 (D) ARGUMENT. SUSTAINED FOR UNCERTAINTY UNDER C.C.P. SECTION 430.10( F) WITH LEAVE TO AMEND IN TEN DAYS TO PLEAD WHETHER THE ALLEGATIONS IN PARAGRAPH V RELATE TO ONE OR BOTH ACTIONS. =(302/PJM/AP)
